one_line_ask: Restore one faded family-under-a-maple-tree photo: clip the two corner-intruding fingers out of frame, recover faithful colour/tone without touching faces, outpaint the canopy and scene for a balanced large-format composition, and deliver a print-ready PNG master plus high-res TIFF and a JPG preview.
brief:
The client supplies ONE scanned family photograph (people seated/standing under a mature maple tree, blue sky, surrounding landscape) that has faded and slightly yellowed with age and has two fingers of the person who held the print intruding into two OPPOSITE corners. They want it digitally restored so it can be professionally printed in large format and proudly displayed. Hard rules from the client: faces, expressions, body shape, age and appearance must stay 100% faithful to the original (no alterations whatsoever); colours must read true to the original; the corner intrusions and any minor distractions must become invisible; and the canopy plus surrounding scene should be subtly EXTENDED to give a more balanced composition for larger-format printing. Mandatory deliverables: a high-resolution print-ready PNG, a high-resolution TIFF, and a smaller JPG preview for quick sharing.

Honest Adobe-connector workflow (every step tagged with execution mode; all server="adobe" except the one final local packaging step):
1. Bring the client scan into Adobe via the file picker (asset_add_file) → presigned URL + assetId. [C]
2. Read the scan with asset_inline_preview to locate the two finger
